# PRD: Chat Reliability Fix Bundle

**Generated by:** Lopmon (CLM Studios)
**Date:** 2026-04-20 (EDT 2026-04-19 evening)
**Status:** Approved by Citlali — full lead given to Lopmon for overnight execution
**Requested by:** Citlali (smoke-test findings #2, #3, #3b) — Z demo imminent

---

## 1. Goal

Make the archetype chat experience actually usable. Today it has three serious defects:
1. The dashboard side-panel chat returns a hardcoded stub response
2. The full-page chat's input locks up (greys out) after any click, requiring page reload
3. Real chat errors are swallowed and show the user an uninformative "I'm having trouble connecting right now"

Fix all three so Z can demo the product tomorrow.

## 2. Scope

### In scope

#### A. Delete the side-panel chat stub (and its mount)

The side-panel chat (`components/chat-provider.tsx` + `components/chat-panel.tsx`) exists, opens on dashboard "Ask a Question" / archetype click, and returns a hardcoded `setTimeout` stub. Citlali confirmed: the side panel is for demoing purposes and is not the intended real-chat UX. The real chat is the full-page route at `/dashboard/team/[slug]`.

Action:
1. Delete `apps/web/src/components/chat-provider.tsx`
2. Delete `apps/web/src/components/chat-panel.tsx`
3. Remove `<ChatPanelProvider>` and `<ChatPanel />` from `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx`
4. In `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`, change the "Ask a Question" quick action from `onClick={() => openChat("executive_assistant")}` to a Next `<Link href="/dashboard/team/executive_assistant">` (using the slug used by the route). Unify pattern with the other cards.
5. Remove the `useChatPanel` import from dashboard/page.tsx
6. In `apps/web/src/components/sidebar.tsx` — if the "YOUR AI TEAM" list in the sidebar currently calls `openChat(slug)` on archetype click, change it to route to `/dashboard/team/${slug}` instead (investigate + update)
7. Remove any remaining references to `openChat`, `useChatPanel`, `ChatPanelProvider`, `ChatPanel` across the codebase (use `rg`/grep to verify)
8. Do NOT delete `@/components/support/ChatWidget` or `@/components/support/ChatProvider` or `<SupportChatProvider>` — those are the *support chat*, a separate surface.

#### B. Fix the ChatInput focus/disabled bug on full-page chat

In `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/team/[slug]/components/ChatInput.tsx`, the textarea greys out and becomes unresponsive after the user clicks anywhere. Symptom: first landing on the page = typing works; any click = textarea permanently disabled until reload.

Reproduce locally with `npm run dev`. Possible causes to investigate in order:
1. `isDisabled={isTyping}` stays `true` after a failed request. `TeamChatClient.tsx:276-287` — verify the catch branch always runs `finally { setIsTyping(false) }`. It currently does, so this shouldn't be the cause, but confirm.
2. Some outer layout click-handler is toggling state. `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx` — look for any `onClick` on wrapper elements that closes or unmounts something.
3. The `useEffect` at `ChatInput.tsx:25-30` resets `textarea.style.height = "auto"` on every `value` change — on a disabled textarea this might cause a re-render that leaves `disabled=true` applied to the new DOM node.
4. The parent `flex overflow-hidden` at `TeamChatClient.tsx:341` with `height: calc(100vh)` might interact badly with the textarea focus ring.

Fix the root cause, not just the symptom. Don't work around it by forcibly setting `isDisabled` to false — find and correct the actual bug. Document root cause in the commit message.

#### C. Surface real chat errors in TeamChatClient

Currently `TeamChatClient.tsx:276-287` catches ALL errors and shows a generic "I'm having trouble connecting right now. Please try again in a moment." message. That's hostile to debugging.

Replace with:
- Inspect the caught error
- If it's a `Response` / `fetch` error with a status code, include the status and the server's error message (the server returns `{ error: "..." }` JSON — parse and display)
- If it's a network failure (no response), say "network error" explicitly
- If it's an unknown error, log the error message visibly in the assistant bubble
- Keep the bubble friendly but informative: `"Chat failed: 401 Unauthorized — please sign in again"` or `"Chat failed: Google Workspace is not connected. Visit Integrations to connect."` — use the server's own error string when available.

Also: update `apps/web/src/app/dashboard/team/[slug]/api.ts` → `sendMessage()` function to read the response body on error status (4xx/5xx) and throw an `Error` whose message includes both the HTTP status and the parsed server error text. Look at that file to see its current shape.

No catch-all opaque message. The UI should tell the user WHAT is wrong.

### Out of scope (intentionally)

- Redesigning the chat UI
- Adding typing indicators, streaming responses, citation highlighting
- Building a new dashboard chat surface (deleting is enough)
- Fixing the "isDisabled while typing" UX (keep `isDisabled={isTyping}` — that behavior is correct; the bug is about it staying true after errors or clicks)
- Any Google/Supabase config changes
- Anything on `findings-2026-04-19.md` that isn't in the three defects above

## 3. Acceptance Criteria

1. ✅ `components/chat-provider.tsx` and `components/chat-panel.tsx` deleted
2. ✅ `<ChatPanelProvider>`, `<ChatPanel />`, `<NoApiKeyBanner />` imports/mounts removed from `dashboard/layout.tsx` (note: `NoApiKeyBanner` is being handled in the separate Dashboard Polish PRD — DO NOT delete it here if the other agent runs in parallel; coordinate via git)
3. ✅ Dashboard "Ask a Question" card navigates to the full-page chat route, not the side panel
4. ✅ Sidebar "YOUR AI TEAM" archetype click navigates to the full-page chat route (verify by reading `sidebar.tsx`)
5. ✅ Full-page chat input stays responsive: clicking anywhere in the page does NOT grey out the textarea; user can type, click away, click back, continue typing
6. ✅ When `/api/team/[slug]/chat` returns an error, the assistant bubble shows the real error (HTTP status + server message), not "I'm having trouble connecting"
7. ✅ `npm run build` passes with zero type errors
8. ✅ No references to `openChat`, `useChatPanel`, `ChatPanelProvider`, `ChatPanel` (the archetype panel, not support) remain in the codebase
9. ✅ Single commit on `main`, message: `fix: chat reliability (delete side-panel stub, fix input focus, surface real errors)`
10. ✅ SESSION-LOG.md entry with root cause analysis of the focus bug and list of deleted files

## 4. Risks & Open Questions

- **Parallel agent coordination:** A separate agent is running `PRD-dashboard-polish.md` which ALSO modifies `dashboard/layout.tsx` (to remove `NoApiKeyBanner`). Resolve cleanly if there's a merge conflict — both changes are additive removals of imports, should be trivial. DO NOT delete `<NoApiKeyBanner />` in this agent's commit; leave that to the dashboard polish agent. Your scope is the chat-panel and chat-provider.
- **Focus bug root cause unknown until reproduced.** If `npm run dev` local repro doesn't surface the issue, the bug might be Vercel-specific (e.g., caused by a different webpack config on production). If you can't reproduce in dev, document it, and ship the best-guess fix based on the code-read clues above. Do NOT ship a change you can't justify.
- **Support chat vs archetype chat:** Be very careful — `components/support/` has a ChatProvider too. That's the SUPPORT chat (customer-support widget). DO NOT delete it. Your scope is ONLY `components/chat-provider.tsx` + `components/chat-panel.tsx` (the archetype chat panel).

## 5. Estimated Effort

~45-60 min of coding agent time + ~10 min /simplify pass.
